---

output: html_document
date: "2024-11-25"
---


```{r }
library("foreign")
data <- read.arff("bone-marrow.arff")

#write.csv(data, file = "475projdata.csv")
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)


# Create survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = data$survival_time, event = data$survival_status)

# Fit Kaplan-Meier model for overall survival
km_fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ 1)

# Basic survival analysis
fit_disease <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Disease, data = data)
fit_risk <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Riskgroup, data = data)
fit_gender <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Recipientgender, data = data)
fit_age <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Recipientage10, data = data)

# Cox proportional hazards model
cox_model <- coxph(surv_obj ~ Disease + Riskgroup + Donorage35 + 
                   Recipientage10 + HLAmismatch + Stemcellsource + 
                   CD34kgx10d6 + Relapse, data = data)

# Create visualizations
# Overall survival curve
p1 <- ggsurvplot(km_fit,
                 data = data,
                 risk.table = TRUE,
                 risk.table.height = 0.25,
                 title = "Overall Survival",
                 xlab = "Time (days)",
                 ylab = "Survival probability")

# Disease-specific survival
p2 <- ggsurvplot(fit_disease,
                 data = data,
                 pval = TRUE,
                 risk.table = TRUE,
                 risk.table.height = 0.25,
                 title = "Survival by Disease Type",
                 xlab = "Time (days)",
                 ylab = "Survival probability")

# Risk group survival
p3 <- ggsurvplot(fit_risk,
                 data = data,
                 pval = TRUE,
                 risk.table = TRUE,
                 risk.table.height = 0.25,
                 title = "Survival by Risk Group",
                 xlab = "Time (days)",
                 ylab = "Survival probability")

# Perform log-rank tests
log_rank_disease <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ Disease, data = data)
log_rank_risk <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ Riskgroup, data = data)
log_rank_gender <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ Recipientgender, data = data)
log_rank_age <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ Recipientage10, data = data)

# Calculate median survival times
median_survival <- summary(km_fit)$table["median"]
disease_medians <- summary(fit_disease)$table[, "median"]

# Print summary statistics
summary(cox_model)
anova(cox_model)

# Create summary table of key findings
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Overall", levels(factor(data$Disease))),
  Median_Survival = c(median_survival, disease_medians),
  Events = table(data$survival_status)[2],
  Total = nrow(data)
)

```


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(survival)
library(dplyr)

# Function to perform univariate Cox regression
perform_univariate_cox <- function(data) {
  # List of predictor variables (excluding survival_time and survival_status)
  predictors <- setdiff(names(data), c("survival_time", "survival_status"))
  
  # Store results
  results <- data.frame(
    variable = character(),
    hazard_ratio = numeric(),
    conf_int_lower = numeric(),
    conf_int_upper = numeric(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Perform univariate Cox regression for each predictor
  for (var in predictors) {
    # Handle missing data
    complete_data <- data[!is.na(data[[var]]), ]
    
    # Create formula
    formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~", var))
    
    # Fit Cox model
    tryCatch({
      cox_model <- coxph(formula, data = complete_data)
      
      # Extract results
      coef <- summary(cox_model)$coefficients[1, ]
      conf_int <- exp(confint(cox_model))
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        variable = var,
        hazard_ratio = exp(coef["coef"]),
        conf_int_lower = conf_int[1],
        conf_int_upper = conf_int[2],
        p_value = coef["Pr(>|z|)"],
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("Error in processing variable:", var))
    })
  }
  
  # Sort results by p-value
  results <- results[order(results$p_value), ]
  
  return(results)
}

# Perform analysis
cox_results <- perform_univariate_cox(data)

# Add significance levels
cox_results$significance <- ifelse(cox_results$p_value < 0.001, "***",
                          ifelse(cox_results$p_value < 0.01, "**",
                          ifelse(cox_results$p_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))

# Print formatted results
print_formatted_results <- function(results) {
  cat("\nUnivariate Cox Regression Results\n")
  cat("================================\n\n")
  
  formatted_results <- data.frame(
    Variable = results$variable,
    "Hazard Ratio" = sprintf("%.2f", results$hazard_ratio),
    "95% CI" = sprintf("(%.2f-%.2f)", results$conf_int_lower, results$conf_int_upper),
    "P-value" = sprintf("%.3f %s", results$p_value, results$significance)
  )
  
  print(formatted_results, row.names = FALSE)
}

print_formatted_results(cox_results)

# Create forest plot for significant variables
library(forestplot)

# Select significant variables (p < 0.05)
sig_results <- subset(cox_results, p_value < 0.05)

# Create forest plot
forestplot(
  labeltext = sig_results$variable,
  mean = log(sig_results$hazard_ratio),
  lower = log(sig_results$conf_int_lower),
  upper = log(sig_results$conf_int_upper),
  zero = 0,
  xlab = "Log Hazard Ratio",
  title = "Forest Plot of Significant Predictors"
)
```

```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(survival)
library(survminer)

# Ensure the dataset is loaded
# Replace 'data' with your dataset name

# Select the variables for the Cox PH model
cox_variables <- c("PLTrecovery", "ANCrecovery", "Rbodymass", "Relapse", 
                   "extcGvHD", "CD3dkgx10d8", "Recipientage", "aGvHDIIIIV", 
                   "CD34kgx10d6")

# Ensure the data does not contain missing values
data <- na.omit(data)

# Define the survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = data$survival_time, event = data$survival_status)

# Fit the Cox Proportional Hazards model
cox_model <- coxph(as.formula(paste("surv_obj ~", paste(cox_variables, collapse = " + "))), data = data)

# Display the summary of the model
summary(cox_model)

# Test proportional hazards assumption
ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_model)
print(ph_test)

# Create diagnostic plots
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(ph_test)


# Create residual plots
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model, type = "martingale")
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model, type = "deviance")



```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries

library(survival)
library(survminer)


cox_unstratified_model <- coxph(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ 
                                Relapse + PLTrecovery + ANCrecovery + 
                                Rbodymass + extcGvHD + CD3dkgx10d8 + 
                                Recipientage + aGvHDIIIIV + CD34kgx10d6, 
                                data = data)

# Fit a Cox model with Relapse as strata
cox_stratified_model <- coxph(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ 
                              strata(Relapse) + 
                              PLTrecovery + ANCrecovery + Rbodymass + 
                              strata(extcGvHD) + CD3dkgx10d8 + Recipientage + 
                              aGvHDIIIIV + CD34kgx10d6, 
                              data = data)

# Load necessary libraries
library(survival)

# Dynamically create a formula excluding survival_time, survival_status, and Relapse
all_vars <- setdiff(names(data), c("survival_time", "survival_status"))
cox_formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ ."))

# Fit a Cox model with Relapse as strata
cox_model <- coxph(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ ., data = data)

# Summarize the model
summary(cox_model)


# Display the summary of the model
summary(cox_model)

# # Test proportional hazards assumption
# ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_model)
# print(ph_test)

# Create diagnostic plots
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(ph_test)


# Create residual plots
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model, type = "martingale")
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model, type = "deviance")



# Fit a Cox model with Relapse as strata
cox_model1 <- coxph(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ 
                       PLTrecovery + ANCrecovery + 
                      Rbodymass + CD3dkgx10d8 + extcGvHD + aGvHDIIIIV +
                      Relapse + HLAmatch + DonorCMV + Donorage + Stemcellsource,
                    data = data)

# Display the summary of the model
summary(cox_model1)


# Create diagnostic plots
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(ph_test)


# Create residual plots
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model1, type = "martingale")
ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model1, type = "deviance")

 

```


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(survival)
library(MASS)
library(survminer)

data <- na.omit(data)  
# Create the full model with all predictors
# First, create the formula with all variables except survival_time and survival_status
all_vars <- setdiff(names(data), c("survival_time", "survival_status"))
full_formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~", 
                               paste(all_vars, collapse = " + ")))

# Fit the full model
full_model <- coxph(full_formula, data = data)

# Perform stepwise selection using AIC criterion
stepwise_model <- step(full_model, 
                      direction = "both",
                      trace = 1)

# Print summary of the final model
summary_result <- summary(stepwise_model)

# Calculate concordance index
concordance <- summary_result$concordance

# Extract final model coefficients and statistics
final_coefficients <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(coef(stepwise_model)),
  Hazard_Ratio = exp(coef(stepwise_model)),
  CI_Lower = exp(confint(stepwise_model))[,1],
  CI_Upper = exp(confint(stepwise_model))[,2],
  P_value = summary_result$coefficients[,5]
)

# Print detailed results
cat("\nStepwise Cox Regression Results\n")
cat("===============================\n\n")
cat("Model Performance:\n")
cat("Concordance Index:", round(concordance[1], 3), "\n")
cat("(95% CI:", round(concordance[2], 3), "-", round(concordance[3], 3), ")\n\n")

# Create proportional hazards test
ph_test <- cox.zph(stepwise_model)

# Plot diagnostics
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(stepwise_model)
plot(ph_test)

# Print final model variables and their statistics
print(final_coefficients)

# Create visual representation of results
ggforest(stepwise_model, 
         data = data,
         main = "Final Model: Hazard Ratios with 95% CI")

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(flexsurv)

df <- read.arff("bone-marrow.arff")

# Inspect the data
head(df)

# Step 1: Prepare Survival Object
# time: survival time
# status: censoring status (1 = event occurred, 0 = censored)
# We'll use 'sex' as a grouping variable (1 = male, 2 = female)

# Create a survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = df$survival_time, event = df$survival_status)

# Step 2: Plot Kaplan-Meier Curves
# Plot KM curves stratified by sex
km_fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Relapse, data = df)

# Basic KM plot
ggsurvplot(km_fit, data = df, pval = TRUE,
           legend.labs = c("Male", "Female"),
           xlab = "Time in days", ylab = "Survival probability",
           title = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Relapse")

# Step 3: Fit Parametric Survival Models

# Fit models with different distributions
models <- list(
  exponential = flexsurvreg(surv_obj ~ Relapse, data = df, dist = "exponential"),
  weibull = flexsurvreg(surv_obj ~ Relapse, data = df, dist = "weibull"),
  lognormal = flexsurvreg(surv_obj ~ Relapse, data = df, dist = "lognormal"),
  loglogistic = flexsurvreg(surv_obj ~ Relapse, data = df, dist = "llogis")
)

# Step 4: Compare Models Using AIC
aic_values <- sapply(models, AIC)
print(aic_values)

# Select the model with the lowest AIC
best_model_name <- names(which.min(aic_values))
best_model <- models[[best_model_name]]

cat("Best-fitting model:", best_model_name, "\n")

# Step 5: Diagnostic Plots
# Plot the fitted survival curve from the best model against the KM curve
plot(km_fit, conf.int = FALSE, col = "blue",
     main = paste("KM Curve vs.", best_model_name, "Model"),
     xlab = "Time in days", ylab = "Survival probability")
lines(best_model, col = "red", lwd = 2)
legend("bottomleft", legend = c("KM Estimate", paste(best_model_name, "Model")),
       col = c("blue", "red"), lwd = 2)

# Optional: Check model diagnostics
# For example, plot residuals or perform goodness-of-fit tests


```
